name: Product Requirements Document
description: Draft, normalize, and slice a product requirements document into Ralph-style executable stories.

fields.name: summary
fields.placeholder: What product capability should be specified?

fields.name: source_notes
fields.description: Raw requirements, links, constraints, or notes
fields.placeholder: Paste product notes, acceptance criteria, constraints, or relevant links.

fields.name: quality_commands
fields.description: Commands each story should pass before completion
fields.placeholder: Example: npm test && npm run lint

steps.name: draft
steps.prompt:
Draft a product requirements document for {{ summary }}.

Use the ticket notes as source material, then inspect the project enough to make the requirements concrete. Write the human-readable PRD to `.tickets/workflows/{{ id }}/prd.md`.

The PRD must include goals, non-goals, users, constraints, risks, acceptance criteria, and a story list. Each story should be small enough for one fresh agent context.

on_reject.prompt:
The PRD draft was rejected: {{ rejection_reason }}

Revise `.tickets/workflows/{{ id }}/prd.md` and tighten the story list before asking for review again.

steps.name: structure
steps.prompt:
Convert `.tickets/workflows/{{ id }}/prd.md` into Ralph-style structured state at `.tickets/workflows/{{ id }}/prd.json`.

The JSON should include an ordered `stories` array. Each story needs a stable id, title, description, acceptance criteria, dependencies, and `passes: false`. Include a `quality_commands` field from the ticket when provided.

Do not mark any story complete yet.

steps.name: slice
steps.prompt:
Review `.tickets/workflows/{{ id }}/prd.json` for story size and execution order.

A good Ralph story can be completed independently by a fresh agent using only the PRD, progress file, repo context, and current ticket. Split any story that is too broad. Make dependencies explicit. If a story is ambiguous, update the PRD and JSON rather than relying on future memory.

on_reject.prompt:
The story slicing review found problems: {{ rejection_reason }}

Revise the PRD and structured story list so each story is independently executable.

steps.name: ready
steps.prompt:
Initialize Ralph loop state for this PRD.

Write `.tickets/workflows/{{ id }}/progress.txt` with: project context, quality commands, current branch, how to select the next story, and any constraints a fresh STORY agent must know. Add a short note explaining that each STORY ticket should complete exactly one story and update `prd.json` plus `progress.txt`.

If useful, create the first STORY ticket for the first incomplete story.
